archetype: Automated Oil Spill Fate Modeling and Response Planning System for Arctic Offshore Drilling Operations
domain: Arctic Offshore Oil and Gas Exploration
description: This system predicts the trajectory and fate of oil spills in Arctic waters, providing critical decision support for offshore drilling operators and response teams. It integrates real-time oceanographic and meteorological data, leveraging machine learning models to forecast oil spill behavior and optimize response strategies. The system must account for the unique challenges of Arctic operations, including sea ice coverage and harsh weather conditions.
rules: MUST adhere to the Oil Spill Response Plan (OSRP) guidelines set by the Bureau of Safety and Environmental Enforcement (BSEE)
MUST incorporate the International Maritime Organization's (IMO) Polar Code requirements for Arctic operations
MUST utilize real-time data from oceanographic and meteorological sensors, including sea ice coverage and currents
MUST account for the properties of oil in cold water, including viscosity and density changes
MUST provide decision support for response teams, including optimal response strategies and resource allocation
anti_patterns: DO NOT rely solely on historical data, as Arctic ocean conditions can be highly variable and unpredictable
DO NOT neglect the impact of sea ice on oil spill behavior and response strategies
DO NOT overlook the need for real-time data integration and model updates to ensure accurate predictions
key_technical_challenges: Developing machine learning models that can accurately predict oil spill behavior in Arctic waters
Integrating real-time data from diverse sources, including oceanographic and meteorological sensors
Optimizing response strategies in a dynamic and uncertain environment

recommended_stack.language: Python - ideal for scientific computing and machine learning tasks
recommended_stack.framework: Flask - suitable for building data-driven web applications
recommended_stack.database_engine: PostgreSQL - supports spatial data types and efficient querying
recommended_stack.cache: Redis - for caching frequently accessed data and reducing database queries
recommended_stack.message_broker: RabbitMQ - for integrating with external data sources and services
special_services: National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) API
